error.signature: torch.jit.frontend.UnsupportedNodeError: Set is not supported in TorchScript
error.regex: (UnsupportedNodeError|not supported in TorchScript|torch\.jit.*not.*support|TorchScript.*unsupported|torch\.jit\.script.*error|cannot be converted to TorchScript)

verdict.summary: Python construct or PyTorch operation not supported in TorchScript. TorchScript supports a limited subset of Python.

dead_ends.action: Use torch.jit.trace instead of torch.jit.script to avoid all errors
dead_ends.why_fails: Trace only captures one execution path; control flow, dynamic shapes, and data-dependent branches are lost

dead_ends.action: Wrap unsupported code in try/except to catch scripting errors
dead_ends.why_fails: TorchScript compilation is ahead-of-time; try/except does not help with compilation failures

workarounds.action: Refactor unsupported Python features to TorchScript-compatible equivalents
workarounds.how: Replace set() with List, dict comprehensions with loops, f-strings with string concatenation

@torch.jit.ignore
def debug_print(self, x): ...  # excluded from TorchScript, only callable in Python

workarounds.action: Switch to torch.compile() (PyTorch 2.0+) instead of TorchScript
workarounds.how: model = torch.compile(model)  # supports full Python, better performance than TorchScript
